articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in complex multi-step reasoning benchmarks and reduced hallucination rates. Enterprise API availability began rolling out in early April with tiered pricing based on context utilization.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Manipulation in Unstructured Environments
articles.summary: Google DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics program, demonstrating foundation models that allow robotic arms to perform complex manipulation tasks in cluttered, novel environments without task-specific fine-tuning. The system leverages a vision-language-action architecture that generalizes across different robot morphologies. Several logistics and warehouse partners have begun pilot integrations.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Constitutional Steering: A New Approach to Real-Time AI Alignment
articles.summary: Anthropic unveiled a technique called Constitutional Steering that allows runtime adjustment of model behavior constraints without retraining, using lightweight activation-space interventions guided by natural language principles. The method reportedly reduces alignment tax on capability benchmarks to under 2% while maintaining robust safety guardrails. The research has been submitted for peer review and the tooling is available in their latest Claude API release.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Reveal Atlas Production Line for Commercial Deployment
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics, backed by Hyundai, announced the opening of a dedicated production facility for its electric Atlas humanoid robot aimed at automotive manufacturing and logistics applications. The commercial Atlas units feature improved battery life of 4+ hours under load, enhanced dexterous manipulation, and an integrated fleet management platform. Initial deployments to Hyundai and KIA factories are scheduled for Q3 2026.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm Gate-All-Around Process Node
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed that its A14 (1.4nm-class) process using gate-all-around nanosheet transistors has entered volume production at its Kaohsiung fab, with initial capacity allocated to Apple and NVIDIA. The node offers approximately 20% performance improvement and 30% power reduction over N2, critical for next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs. Yield rates are reportedly ahead of schedule compared to the N2 ramp at the same stage.

articles.title: Meta Releases Open-Source Llama 4 Behemoth with Mixture-of-Experts Architecture at 2 Trillion Parameters
articles.summary: Meta released the full weights and training documentation for Llama 4 Behemoth, a 2-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model that activates roughly 200 billion parameters per forward pass. The model sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple academic and industry benchmarks while remaining runnable on multi-node GPU clusters accessible to well-funded research labs. The permissive license continues Meta's strategy of commoditizing the model layer of the AI stack.

articles.title: Breakthrough Solid-State Battery from QuantumScape Achieves 600 Wh/kg in Production-Intent Cells
articles.summary: QuantumScape announced that its latest lithium-metal solid-state cells have achieved 600 Wh/kg energy density at the cell level in production-intent form factors, nearly doubling the density of current top-tier lithium-ion cells. The cells retained over 80% capacity after 1,000 fast-charge cycles, addressing a long-standing durability concern. Automotive OEM partners including Volkswagen have confirmed integration into 2028 model-year vehicle platforms.

articles.title: Figure AI Secures $1.5B Series C to Scale Humanoid Robot Deployment in BMW and Amazon Facilities
articles.summary: Figure AI closed a $1.5 billion Series C funding round at a $15 billion valuation, with investment from Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Amazon's Industrial Innovation Fund. The capital will fund scaling production of its Figure 02 humanoid to thousands of units for deployment at BMW's Spartanburg plant and Amazon fulfillment centers. The robots use on-device vision-language models for task understanding, enabling non-expert operators to assign work via natural language commands.
